posts.hook: 75% of people need glasses by 40. Most of them could have slowed it down.
posts.caption:
Myopia is now an epidemic.

- 1 in 3 people globally are nearsighted
- By 2050: projected to be 1 in 2
- Screen time is the #1 modifiable risk factor

The good news: you can slow it down.

Structured eye exercises + screen breaks = measurable difference.

Free tools at axeo.vision

#Myopia #EyeHealth #Axeo #AxeoVision #VisionCare #ScreenTime #EyeExercise

posts.video_prompt: Runway Gen-4: Animated stat cards on dark background (#0D0E14). NO PEOPLE. CARD 1: bold teal (#00C9A7) '1 IN 3' counts up, white subtitle 'globally nearsighted'. CARD 2: '1 IN 2' in teal, subtitle 'projected by 2050'. CARD 3: 'SCREEN TIME' in amber, subtitle '#1 modifiable risk factor'. Each card: 0.3s scale-in, 2s hold, hard-cut. Between cards: macro shot of a phone screen glowing cold blue in darkness (no person). End: Axeo teal logo on dark background, 1.5s hold. Urgent, clinical tone. 9:16 vertical. Total: 14 seconds.

posts.hook: The 20-20-20 rule — but the version that actually works.
posts.caption:
You've heard of 20-20-20. Most people do it wrong.

The real version:

Every 20 minutes →
Look at something 20 FEET away (not across the room — outside a window)
For 20 seconds (count out loud)
+ Blink 5 times deliberately

The blink reset matters. Blink rate drops 80% on screens.

Set a real timer. Do it now.

Free reminders built into Axeo: axeo.vision

#2020Rule #EyeStrain #Axeo #AxeoVision #DigitalEyeStrain #EyeHealth #OfficeLife
posts.visual_notes: Step-by-step tutorial style. Show person at desk, timer going off, demonstrating the proper 20-20-20. Emphasize the window/outdoor gaze and blink count. Clean instructional aesthetic.
posts.video_prompt: Runway Gen-4: Three-part sequence. NO PEOPLE throughout. PART 1 (8s): cinematic wide shot from inside a dark room looking through a large window at a bright green park. Soft focus on window frame, sharp on outdoor scene. A countdown timer UI overlay reads '20 seconds' and ticks down. Slow gentle zoom out from window. PART 2 (7s): medical illustration of a human eye's ciliary muscle (teal highlight on dark navy background) slowly releasing tension — the muscle visibly unclenches. Subtle orange glow fades as tension releases. PART 3 (5s): clean desk surface with phone showing axeo.vision, warm ambient light. Calm, restorative mood throughout. 9:16. 20 seconds total.

posts.hook: The 20-20-20 rule most people get wrong.
posts.caption:
20-20-20: Every 20 min, look 20 feet away, for 20 seconds.

The problem: most people look at the wall 5 feet away.

You need to look OUTSIDE. Out a window. To reset your ciliary muscle (the one that cramps from near focus).

Free reminder system: axeo.vision

#2020Rule #EyeStrain #Axeo #EyeHealth #HealthTok #WorkFromHome #OfficeLife
posts.visual_notes: Split screen: person looking at wall (wrong) vs window (right). Anatomy diagram of ciliary muscle. Educational energy. Quick cuts.

posts.hook: Myopia doubled in 25 years. Here's why — and how to slow it.
posts.caption:
Why is myopia doubling every generation?

Researchers have two theories:
1. Near work hypothesis — close focus for hours strains the eye's growth
2. Outdoor light theory — UV light triggers proper eye development; we get too little

Both are affected by our screen-heavy lives.

What helps: structured breaks, outdoor time, guided exercises.

Axeo's programs are built around this research.

Free at axeo.vision

#Myopia #VisionScience #Axeo #EyeHealth #IndoorGeneration #Nearsighted
posts.visual_notes: Scientific explanation style. Diagram of myopic eye elongation. Research stats. Hopeful ending showing preventive strategies.

posts.hook: Myopia rates doubled in 25 years. By 2050 it'll affect half of all humans.
posts.caption:
Myopia rates doubled in 25 years. By 2050 it could affect 50% of humans.

The main driver: more time indoors, less outdoor light, more near-work.

Screens didn't cause myopia. But they accelerate it.

Slowing it down: axeo.vision/exercise
